title: DES module overview with Cipher API binding
code:
module DES where

import Cipher

// DES API
DES : Cipher 64 64
DES =
    { encrypt key pt = des pt (expandKey key)
    , decrypt key ct = des ct (reverse (expandKey key))
    }
explanation: Defines the top-level DES value as a Cipher record with encrypt/decrypt fields. Encryption uses `des pt (expandKey key)` and decryption reverses the expanded round keys.
retrieval_text: DES module overview. Binds DES as Cipher 64 64 with encrypt using des plus expandKey, and decrypt using des with reversed expanded keys.

title: DES core encryption pipeline with initial and final permutations
code:
des pt keys = (swap (split lst)) @@ FPz
  where
        pt' = pt @@ IPz
        iv  = [ round (k, split lr)
              | k  <- keys
              | lr <- [pt'] # iv ]
        lst = iv @ (length keys - 1)
explanation: Applies the initial permutation, iterates Feistel rounds using a self-referential running sequence `iv`, then swaps halves and applies the final permutation.
retrieval_text: DES core function. Uses initial permutation pt @@ IPz, iterates round over keys with a self-referential sequence, then swap split halves and apply final permutation @@ FPz.

title: Single DES Feistel round
explanation: A classic Feistel round: return the old right half as the new left half, and XOR the old left half with the round function output to form the new right half.
retrieval_text: Single DES Feistel round. Takes a 48-bit round key and two 32-bit halves, returns r # (l ^ f (r, k)). Useful as a Feistel round template.

title: DES f-function using expansion, key mixing, S-boxes, and permutation
explanation: Implements the DES f-function by expanding the right half with EPz, XORing with the round key, passing through SBox, then permuting with PPz.
retrieval_text: DES f-function pipeline: expand right half with @@ EPz, xor with round key, run SBox, then apply permutation @@ PPz.

title: DES round-key expansion with scheduled rotations
explanation: For each cumulative rotation amount, rotates the left and right 28-bit halves, concatenates them, and selects the compressed round key bits using CPz.
retrieval_text: DES expand function. For each cumulative shift amount, rotate both 28-bit halves with <<<, concatenate them, then select round-key bits via @@ CPz.

title: Running cumulative sums via self-referential recurrence
code:
sums : [16][8] -> [16][8]
sums xs = ys
  where ys = [ x + y | x <- xs | y <- [0] # ys ]
explanation: Builds cumulative shift amounts with a self-referential list comprehension. This is a compact recurrence pattern for prefix sums.
retrieval_text: Running cumulative sums in Cryptol using a self-referential sequence: ys = [ x + y | x <- xs | y <- [0] # ys ]. Useful for scheduled rotations and prefix accumulators.

title: Convert 1-based specification tables to 0-based Cryptol indices
code:
zeroBasify XP = [ i - 1 | i <- XP ]
KPz = zeroBasify KP
CPz = zeroBasify CP
PPz = zeroBasify PP
EPz = zeroBasify EP
IPz = zeroBasify IP
FPz = zeroBasify FP
explanation: Subtracts 1 from each element in a specification table so it can be used directly with Cryptol indexing and permutation operators.
retrieval_text: Convert specification tables from 1-based numbering to 0-based Cryptol indices using [ i - 1 | i <- XP ]. Useful when porting NIST-style permutation tables.

title: DES permutation tables
explanation: Static permutation and expansion tables used throughout DES: key permutation, compression permutation, P permutation, expansion permutation, initial permutation, and final permutation.
retrieval_text: DES permutation tables: KP, CP, PP, EP, IP, FP. Useful as examples of static cryptographic tables later normalized to zero-based indices and applied with @@.
